destination.comments: Reads ALL PENDING sftp jobs (no LIMIT) within the 13-min freshness window (SP wait 15m - 2m load buffer). Max Rows Per Flow File=1 emits one FlowFile per job so the whole backlog drains in a single run. Stale requests (>13m, graph run already gave up) are skipped so Bronze is never loaded without a Silver run following; the reaper FAILs them. PARAMS VARIANT flattened via Snowflake colon-extraction.
destination.name: ReadPendingJob

source.comments: 1-day schedule so it never auto-fires. Woken on demand via PUT /nifi-api/processors/{id}/run-status with state=RUN_ONCE.
source.name: Trigger_Entry_Point

destination.comments: Routes to Success (no_job) when queue is empty, or continues (unmatched) when a job row exists.
destination.name: CheckIfHasJob

source.comments: Reads ALL PENDING sftp jobs (no LIMIT) within the 13-min freshness window (SP wait 15m - 2m load buffer). Max Rows Per Flow File=1 emits one FlowFile per job so the whole backlog drains in a single run. Stale requests (>13m, graph run already gave up) are skipped so Bronze is never loaded without a Silver run following; the reaper FAILs them. PARAMS VARIANT flattened via Snowflake colon-extraction.
source.name: ReadPendingJob

source.comments: Replaces Bronze table each run (truncate+load). Reads header, creates all-VARCHAR columns + a FILENAME lineage column (only if the CSV doesn't already have one), and builds the matching transform COPY (METADATA$FILENAME -> FILENAME) into BRONZE_COPY_SQL.
source.name: CreateBronzeTable

controllerServices.comments: Shared JDBC connection pool. Parameters come from Queue_SFTP parameter context.
controllerServices.name: SnowflakeConnectionService

controllerServices.comments: Writes ExecuteSQLRecord output as JSON array.
controllerServices.name: JsonRecordSetWriter

processors.comments: 1-day schedule so it never auto-fires. Woken on demand via PUT /nifi-api/processors/{id}/run-status with state=RUN_ONCE.
processors.name: Trigger_Entry_Point

processors.comments: Reads ALL PENDING sftp jobs (no LIMIT) within the 13-min freshness window (SP wait 15m - 2m load buffer). Max Rows Per Flow File=1 emits one FlowFile per job so the whole backlog drains in a single run. Stale requests (>13m, graph run already gave up) are skipped so Bronze is never loaded without a Silver run following; the reaper FAILs them. PARAMS VARIANT flattened via Snowflake colon-extraction.
processors.name: ReadPendingJob

properties.SQL Query:
SELECT JOB_ID, SOURCE_ID, SOURCE_TYPE, TARGET_DATABASE, TARGET_SCHEMA, TARGET_TABLE,
       PARAMS:sftp_host::VARCHAR     AS SFTP_HOST,
       PARAMS:sftp_port::VARCHAR     AS SFTP_PORT,
       PARAMS:sftp_username::VARCHAR AS SFTP_USERNAME,
       PARAMS:sftp_password::VARCHAR AS SFTP_PASSWORD,
       PARAMS:remote_path::VARCHAR   AS REMOTE_PATH,
       PARAMS:filename::VARCHAR      AS FILENAME,
       PARAMS:stage::VARCHAR         AS TARGET_STAGE
FROM MEDALLION_DW.CONTROL.INGEST_QUEUE
WHERE STATUS = 'PENDING' AND SOURCE_TYPE = 'sftp'
  AND CREATED_AT > DATEADD(minute, -13, SYSDATE())
ORDER BY CREATED_AT

processors.comments: Routes to Success (no_job) when queue is empty, or continues (unmatched) when a job row exists.
processors.name: CheckIfHasJob

processors.comments: Extracts job fields from JSON array row into FlowFile attributes. Uses $[0].XXX because ExecuteSQLRecord outputs a JSON array.
processors.name: ExtractJobParams

processors.comments: Marks job RUNNING so a second trigger does not pick up the same job.
processors.name: MarkRunning

properties.SQL Statement: UPDATE MEDALLION_DW.CONTROL.INGEST_QUEUE SET STATUS = 'RUNNING' WHERE JOB_ID = '${JOB_ID}'

processors.comments: Fetches the exact file named in FILENAME from the SFTP server.
processors.name: FetchFromSFTP

processors.comments: Uploads the fetched file to the Bronze internal stage.
processors.name: StageFile

processors.comments: Replaces Bronze table each run (truncate+load). Reads header, creates all-VARCHAR columns + a FILENAME lineage column (only if the CSV doesn't already have one), and builds the matching transform COPY (METADATA$FILENAME -> FILENAME) into BRONZE_COPY_SQL.
processors.name: CreateBronzeTable

processors.comments: Runs the transform COPY built by CreateBronzeTable (adds FILENAME = source file name when the source doesn't already provide one).

processors.name: MarkDone

properties.SQL Statement: UPDATE MEDALLION_DW.CONTROL.INGEST_QUEUE SET STATUS = 'DONE', PROCESSED_AT = SYSDATE() WHERE JOB_ID = '${JOB_ID}'

processors.comments: Marks job FAILED — SP_TRIGGER_OPENFLOW polling loop exits with error.
processors.name: MarkFailed

properties.SQL Statement: UPDATE MEDALLION_DW.CONTROL.INGEST_QUEUE SET STATUS = 'FAILED', PROCESSED_AT = SYSDATE() WHERE JOB_ID = '${JOB_ID}'

processors.comments: Empties FlowFile content after the file is staged in Snowflake. Downstream uses attributes only, so this keeps the Create/Copy/MarkDone queues tiny under concurrency.
processors.name: DropContentAfterStage
